mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which directly supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows consistent positive month-over-month growth, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate is maintained at extremely low levels, it signals an aggressively accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and encouraging investment, thereby boosting equity valuations over a longer, quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05) during periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies robust consumer demand and financial health, translating into higher corporate revenues and profits, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of instances when both RSAFS growth is strong and UNRATE is low, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During an economic expansion, sustained increases in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect growing manufacturing and mining output, indicating healthy business activity and capacity utilization, which positively impacts corporate earnings and S&P 500 performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth does not correlate positively with S&P 500 60-day returns during confirmed periods of economic expansion, with a Rank IC below 0.05, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and significant increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a severe deterioration in the labor market, indicating an impending or ongoing economic contraction that leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances following a 0.5pp rise in UNRATE over 3 months, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive momentum in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong builder confidence and demand for new homes, acting as a leading indicator for broader economic activity and consumer wealth, which supports short-term positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: If periods of sustained positive HOUST growth do not lead to positive S&P 500 5-day returns, with a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of high consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and contracting retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a stagflationary environment where purchasing power is eroded and demand is falling, leading to significant downward pressure on corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of instances where CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% and RSAFS MoM < 0%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a robust economic growth environment, as indicated by strong GDP, further confirmation from accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) signals exceptionally strong consumer and business confidence, which translates into superior corporate performance and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p < 0.05) during periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show strong year-over-year growth, the hypothesis is falsified.
